function.name: bash
function.description: Execute a bash command. For commands that may take more than 2 seconds (network requests, builds, package installs, recursive file searches, compilation), you MUST set run_in_background=true. When run_in_background is true, the command runs asynchronously and the result will be delivered to you automatically when ready. Respond to the user acknowledging the request is being processed.

run_in_background.description: MUST be true for commands that may take more than 2 seconds: network requests (curl, wget), builds (cargo build, npm install, make), recursive searches on large directories, package management (pip, brew), and any command with unpredictable execution time. Defaults to false.

timeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds for synchronous commands. Defaults to 120000 (2 minutes).
